name: lighthouse_automation_coast_guard_channel_conflict
premise: Coast Guard district chief Casey Garcia must decide whether to approve automated lighthouse dimming that reduces bird mortality by an estimated fourteen percent but interferes with legacy analog distress receivers used by small-boat fishers in fog. Electronics engineer Hugo Zedillo modeled interference spikes on channel sixteen during modulation tests. Keeping legacy brightness preserves analog reliability but kills more migrating seabirds. The international aids-to-navigation committee meets tomorrow; tonight's fog forecast is severe.

agents.name: Casey Garcia
agents.identity: I am the district chief responsible for aids-to-navigation compliance, mariner safety in fog, and environmental mitigation commitments under bird-protection settlements.
agents.goal: I want zero collision losses without silently degrading distress reception for fishers who cannot afford digital radios yet.

agents.name: Hugo Zedillo
agents.identity: I am the electronics engineer who measured analog interference during dimming cycles and who recommends phased hardware subsidies for digital upgrades.
agents.goal: I want dimming delayed until every high-risk fishing cooperative receives subsidized receivers, even if that delays bird gains another season.

choices: I will approve dimming tonight with broadcast warnings and temporary guard ships on the worst fog routes, accepting fuel costs and partial analog interference risk.
I will deny dimming until subsidized digital receiver rollout hits eighty percent adoption in the cooperative census, accepting higher bird mortality this migration window.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Fog advisories are upgrading and fishers are monitoring channel sixteen. Casey Garcia must choose between approving dimming with guard ships or denying until receiver rollout. What does Casey Garcia do?
